ogTitle: Port Noarlunga South vs Port Paterson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Noarlunga South and Port Paterson.
heading: Port Noarlunga South vs Port Paterson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Noarlunga South, South Australia and Port Paterson, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Noarlunga South has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Port Paterson (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Noarlunga South or Port Paterson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Noarlunga South has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Port Paterson scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Noarlunga South scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Port Paterson scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Noarlunga South or Port Paterson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Noarlunga South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
